title: node-postgres Pool, Client Ownership, Transactions, and Shutdown · Transfer Build
summary: An independent university archive receives a different publisher mix, unknown XML shape, validator state, tenant, async schedule, database condition, accessibility need, platform, and injected failure and transfers node-postgres pool, client ownership, transactions, and shutdown into a changed bounded pg repository and transaction port. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of bounded pg repository and transaction port with automated and judgment evidence. Learners pr…
objectives: Create one bounded Pool, handle background errors, observe acquire waits, reject work during closure, and await pool.end after workers settle.
Release every successfully acquired client in finally, preserve the original error, and test error, cancellation, and shutdown paths.
Run BEGIN, every statement, COMMIT or ROLLBACK on the same checked-out client and prohibit pool.query inside the unit of work.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Ts Pool Lifecycle
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab TypeScript team handles case 1943 during a hostile XML and unknown-value admission drill. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove Pool, checked-out client, transaction connection, row validation, error, and close ownership; the release claim must bind source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, migrations, lockfile, package, image, configuration, and restored state; then change one tenant, cursor, reader command, authorization, validator, sanitizer, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Create one bounded Pool, handle background errors, observe acquire waits, reject work during closure, and await pool.end after workers settle.
